page: Amazon Web Services is4gen.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by 32 dedicated physical cores of the custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:6 vCPU-to-memory ratio with 6.0 GB per core. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core and memory performance, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 48576.33 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11813.0. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core scores of 971.0 and single-threaded gzip compression at 20.34 GB/sec falling into the weak category. LLM text generation for small models is also poor at 24.55 tokens/sec, though medium model prompt processing and text generation remain average.

Given the massive 30 TB of bundled NVMe SSD storage and 192 GB of RAM, this server offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, as evidenced by an average Passmark database score of 20432.69 and 36.84 million Redis SET operations per second. Other appropriate workloads include static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and distributed file systems.
description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.8xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, a 50 Gbps baseline network, and a massive 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this server delivers average multi-core performance, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 48576.33 and a Passmark database score of 20432.69. However, single-core capabilities represent a tradeoff, with weak single-threaded compression and single-core Geekbench scores. Given its high storage density and solid multi-core throughput, the is4gen.8xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-intensive applications. It is best suited for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated ARM64 Graviton cores, 192 GB of RAM, and 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-density database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.8xlarge is an ARM64 server with 32 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 30 TB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for database workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring thirty terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage and dedicated cores for data-intensive workloads.

30,000 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
192.0 GB of system memory with 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average multi-core performance with 48576.33 stress-ng score
Weaker single-core and single-threaded execution speeds
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis, and static web serving
